commit.message:
fix: signal delay now used by 1PN VV correction, PE, and Darwin energy

Three paths were using instantaneous positions while relativity was on:

- compute1PNPairwise(): velocity-Verlet correction after drift now uses
  getDelayedState() unconditionally (1PN requires relativity, so signal
  delay is always active when this function runs)

- computePE() / treePE(): potential energy computation now uses retarded
  positions at leaf/pairwise level, current-time aggregates for distant
  BH nodes — same hybrid approach as forces and heatmap

- computeEnergies() Pass 3: Darwin field energy & momentum correction
  now uses retarded source positions/velocities

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(B3): add closer-bot insert warning toast

active for a Revio contact. Toast auto-dismisses after 4s, resets on

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Remove all normalization layers; fix per-bot OpenClaw session routing

- Delete session key normalization (_normalize_session_key) from ingest
  pipeline, bridge, and service — raw keys like "agent:main:main" are
  used everywhere now
- Delete OPENCLAW_WS_SESSIONS env var — bridge derives session keys
  from /v1/bots API (DB is single source of truth)
- Delete color alias normalization (_COLOR_ALIASES) from Bot and
  RuntimeSettingsManager — colors stored as-is
- Consolidate bot color and default_voice from runtime settings into
  bot_profiles table with proper migration
- Fix per-bot session routing: override AgentBackendClient._bot_config
  in _init_bot() after self.bot is available, so each openclaw bot
  uses its own session_key instead of sharing the first-registered one
- Fix profile upsert clobbering nullable fields (agent_backend, color,
  etc.) by using "is not None" guards
- Fix auto-push SOUL.md: await directly instead of create_task, pass
  plain strings instead of profile object

commit.message:
fix: remove const from constructor call sites after adding assert() validation #121 #122 (#142)

* feat: add equality, hashCode, and constructor validation to all models #121 #122

Implement operator== and hashCode for all model classes:
ActualExpense, CategoryBudgetSummary, MonthlyBudget, SavingsGoal,
SavingsContribution, ShoppingItem, Product, ExpenseSnapshot,
CoachInsight, RecurringExpense, OnboardingState, SubscriptionState,
CommandAction, BudgetCategoryView, PurchaseRecord, CustomReminder,
NotificationPreferences.

Add assert validations for monetary fields:
- ActualExpense.amount >= 0
- MonthlyBudget.amount >= 0
- SavingsGoal.targetAmount >= 0, currentAmount >= 0
- SavingsContribution.amount >= 0
- ShoppingItem.price >= 0
- RecurringExpense.amount >= 0
- NotificationPreferences.budgetAlertThreshold 0-100

Add missing toJson() to Product and ExpenseSnapshot models.

Fixes #121
Fixes #122

https://claude.ai/code/session_01C2YdBDGaZ1nAWVXCed2Afj

* fix: remove const from constructor call sites after adding assert() validation #121 #122

Adding assert() to model constructors (RecurringExpense, SavingsGoal,
NotificationPreferences, etc.) removes the const qualifier in Dart.
This updates all call sites in lib/ and test/ to use non-const construction.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01C2YdBDGaZ1nAWVXCed2Afj

commit.message:
Add collapsible annotation panel with caret toggle (#147)

## Summary
- Add `isCollapsed` / `onToggleCollapsed` props to `AnnotationPanel` for
collapse/expand toggle
- When collapsed, panel shrinks to a 24px strip with a chevron caret to
re-expand
- Smooth 200ms CSS width transition on collapse/expand
- Both left and right panels get independent toggles
- Resize divider hidden when adjacent panel is collapsed
- Accessible with `aria-label` on toggle buttons

## Test Plan
- [x] 4 new unit tests for collapse toggle behavior (expanded shows
button, click fires callback, collapsed hides SVG, collapsed click fires
callback)
- [x] All 1896 unit tests pass
- [x] All 39 e2e tests pass
- [x] Build: zero errors
- [x] Lint: zero errors

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

commit.message:
feat: add Authenticode signing helper for AllSigned environments

Adds Helpers\Sign-GRScripts.ps1, adapted from Sign-LZScripts.ps1 in the
AD Landing Zone project. Signs all 12 production .ps1 files in one pass;
-IncludeTests extends coverage to the 5 Pester test scripts.

Key documentation added throughout:
- The invoke workaround (ScriptBlock::Create / Invoke-Expression bypasses
  both ExecutionPolicy and Authenticode) is documented as an anti-pattern
  in the script .DESCRIPTION and README
- Dot-source coverage requirement: every file the deployer dot-sources at
  runtime must be individually signed, not just entry-point orchestrators
- Re-signing requirement after any edit noted in README and V2-Decisions.md
- RFC 3161 timestamp recommendation for production longevity

Updates:
- README.md: new Code Signing section with usage examples and workaround note
- Docs/V2-Decisions.md: design rationale for signing decisions
- Removes Helpers\Sign-LZScripts.ps1 (source project artifact, untracked)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(infra): replace Istio NodePort with EKS Auto Mode ALB

- K8S/alb-ingress.yaml: IngressClass (controller: eks.amazonaws.com/alb)
  + Ingress in dev namespace; scheme=internet-facing, target-type=ip,
  healthcheck on port 15021 (/healthz/ready), idle_timeout=3600s (WebSocket)
- K8S/istio-ingress-svc.yaml: convert istio-ingress from NodePort to
  ClusterIP; ALB uses target-type=ip so NodePort is no longer needed
- terraform.tfvars: node_public_dns → ALB DNS, istio_http_nodeport → 80;
  feeds both CloudFront origin and all API GW HTTP_PROXY integrations

New flow: CloudFront/API GW → ALB:80 → Istio ingress pods (via IP)

feat: Buddhi pre-flight gate — deterministic tool selection + token budget

80% infra: before every LLM call, Buddhi classifies user intent via
regex (zero tokens) and returns only relevant tools. EXPLORE gets 3
tools instead of 6. EXPLAIN excludes write tools. TEST gets bash +
read only.

- TaskIntent enum: EXPLORE, FIX, CREATE, MODIFY, TEST, EXPLAIN, GENERAL
- BuddhiDirective: tool_names (frozenset) + max_tokens constraint
- Phase evolution: EXPLORE gains write tools after round 3
- Error adaptation: 2+ recent errors adds bash for debugging
- 14 new tests (237 total)

Token savings: ~200-400 input tokens per LLM call on focused tasks.

commit.message:
Add procedural texture graph evaluator with lazy rendering

Implement full evaluator for rev 639 procedural texture graphs (index 9).
The evaluator supports all 40 node types using 12-bit fixed-point arithmetic,
rendering texture DAGs to ARGB bitmaps. Textures are rendered lazily on first
access rather than at load time, keeping startup fast. Sprite-based thumbnails
are preferred over graph rendering for model textures until the evaluator
matures.

Key changes:
- TextureGraphEvaluator.cs: new 1200-line evaluator with all node types
- Texture.cs: extended parser to build full node graph with config fields
- TextureManager.cs: lazy EnsureRendered(), sprites-first for GL stability
- RSBufferedImage.cs: GetPixels() for sprite source node sampling
- GLTextureCache.cs: lazy texture rendering on first GL request
- ModelDefinition.cs: fix vertex scaling and FaceTextures mask
- MappedDataChannel.cs: track data length separately from mapped length
- Editor.cs: skip-write optimisation, texture tab diagnostics

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Switch burn-gate auth from hotkey to coldkey

burn_alpha extrinsics are signed by the coldkey, not the hotkey. The
caller stakes TAO to any validator on SN103, then burns alpha from that
position. API requests are now signed with the coldkey (X-Coldkey header)
and the validator matches the extrinsic signer accordingly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

add: ダッシュボードに「応用トレーニング」カテゴリを新設、Java実力養成ドリルを移動

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Classify ImplementsWithParamAssignment → §8.26.2 [skip ci]

The test exercises `class C implements IFace#(int)` — a class implementing a parameterized interface class with explicit parameter assignment. Section 8.26.2 ("Extends versus implements") defines the `implements` keyword semantics and contains examples of parameterized interface class usage (e.g., `implements PutImp#(T), GetImp#(T)`). No more-specific subclause covers this syntax.

commit.message:
Add second Instagram post to Day Three diary entry

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

perf: idle player physics skip + snap phase spatial cell cache

- TickHandler: skip Jolt updatePlayerPhysics for players with no directional
  input, onGround, and near-zero XZ velocity after 1 settling tick. Saves
  ~47µs per idle player per tick via playerIdleCounts Map. At 150p with
  players packed at spawn points (friction decelerates them), 144/150 players
  skip physics per tick, reducing mv from 7ms to 3.6-5.3ms avg.

- TickHandler: snap phase spatialCache groups players by floor(x/R) cell key,
  sharing octree query results for co-located players. Eliminates redundant
  _playerIndex.nearby and getRelevantDynamicIds calls when multiple players
  in same snap group occupy the same spatial cell.

- TickHandler: profile log now reports idle:N count for monitoring physics
  skip effectiveness in production.

- apps/stress-entities: new app for systematic entity count stress testing.
  STRESS_ENTITY_COUNT env var controls spawn count (default 100). Spawns
  box-dynamic entities in a grid pattern.

- CLAUDE.md: updated Performance Verification with measured profiling data
  at 64 TPS (15.6ms budget), bottleneck analysis, and new optimization docs.

Profiled results (64 TPS, 1000 dynamic entities):
  150p all-moving: avg 11.98ms (was 14.34ms, -16%)
  250p half-idle:  avg 16.89ms (was 19.08ms all-moving, -11%)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
